sense.definition: a location where an event or action takes place, such as a meeting or performance

examples.x: The concert will be held at a large outdoor venue.
The venue for the conference is located in the city center.
We need to find a suitable venue for the wedding reception.
The venue was packed with fans for the football game.
They chose a historic venue for their anniversary celebration.

sense.definition: a specialized cycling track, typically with steeply banked curves, designed for track cycling events

examples.x: The cyclists trained at the velodrome to prepare for the upcoming race.
Spectators cheered as the riders zoomed around the velodrome.
They installed new lighting at the velodrome for evening training sessions.
He set a new record for the fastest lap time at the velodrome
The velodrome was filled with the sound of whirring wheels and cheering fans.

sense.definition: a stadium or field where games, especially baseball, are played, equipped with seating for spectators and facilities for players

examples.x: We cheered from the stands at the park during the baseball game.
Players gather at the park for practice sessions.
The team won a crucial game at the local park's field.
Fans filled the park to watch the championship match.
He hit a homer over the park's outfield fence.

sense.definition: a designated area, either indoors or outdoors, where people skate on smooth ice for leisure or competitive purposes

examples.x: She practiced her routines on the skating rink every morning.
The hockey team practiced on the skating rink.
Skaters gathered at the outdoor skating rink to enjoy the winter weather.
The figure skater performed beautifully on the skating rink.
New lighting was installed at the skating rink for night skating.

sense.definition: a specially designed structure that holds water for people to swim in

examples.x: The hotel had a large swimming pool where guests could relax and swim.
We spent the afternoon lounging by the community swimming pool.
My friend's backyard has a small, but refreshing, swimming pool.
The resort's swimming pool overlooked the ocean, offering stunning views.
After work, I like to unwind by taking a dip in the indoor swimming pool.

sense.definition: a course specifically designed for racing events, where human runners, horses, or cars can compete against each other

examples.x: The racetrack was buzzing with excitement as the competitors lined up for the final lap.
She loves going to the racetrack on weekends to watch the races.
The racetrack was closed for maintenance, so the event was rescheduled.
The driver skillfully navigated the sharp turns on the racetrack.
The racetrack was packed with spectators eager to see the big race.

sense.definition: the accommodation area provided for athletes during the Olympic Games

examples.x: Athletes from around the world stay at the Olympic Village during the Games.
The Olympic Village provides housing, dining, and recreational facilities for competitors.
Security is tight around the Olympic Village to ensure the safety of athletes.
Athletes share living quarters with teammates and competitors in the Olympic Village.
After competition, athletes often relax and socialize with fellow competitors in the Olympic Village.

sense.definition: a very large, often roofless, structure where sports events, etc. are held for an audience

examples.x: The new stadium was packed with fans, all eagerly awaiting the kickoff of the championship football game.
Concerts and major sporting events often draw huge crowds to the stadium, creating an electric atmosphere.
The city invested in upgrading the stadium to include modern amenities, such as improved seating and high-definition video screens.
As the sun set, the stadium lights illuminated the field, signaling the start of the evening's baseball game.
The stadium's design allows for excellent acoustics, making it a popular choice for both sports events and live music performances.

sense.definition: a large open-air constructed area that is used for playing sports
a large stadium or arena used for sporting events, often characterized by its size and historical significance

examples.x: The basketball team played their home games at the ancient coliseum.
The gladiator matches were held in the Roman coliseum centuries ago.
She attended the tennis match at the modern coliseum downtown.
The football coliseum was filled with enthusiastic fans cheering for their team.
The coliseum's architecture is a blend of modern amenities and historical charm.

sense.definition: a place with special equipment that people go to exercise or play sports

examples.x: He goes to the gym five times a week.
The gym was crowded with people after work.
She joined a new gym near her house.
They offer yoga classes at the gym.
I saw her lifting weights at the gym yesterday.

sense.definition: a container of water that people can swim in

examples.x: The community center has an indoor pool where families can enjoy swimming together regardless of the weather outside.
During the summer heatwave, many locals flock to the public pool to cool off and relax in the refreshing water.
The hotel boasts a luxurious outdoor pool surrounded by lush tropical gardens, providing guests with a tranquil oasis to unwind.
After a long day at work, Sarah likes to unwind by taking a dip in her backyard pool, enjoying the soothing sensation of the water.
The Olympic-sized pool at the sports complex is used for competitive swimming events and training sessions by professional athletes.

sense.definition: a training facility or space used for martial arts practice and instruction

examples.x: Students bow upon entering the dojo as a sign of respect.
The dojo walls are adorned with traditional martial arts symbols and inspirational quotes.
Beginners are often introduced to basic techniques in the dojo's beginner class.
Black belts serve as mentors to lower-ranked students in the dojo.
Parents watch proudly from the viewing area as their children practice in the dojo.

sense.definition: a building, typically found on a farm, designed to house horses

examples.x: The rancher built a new stable to accommodate the growing number of horses on the farm.

sense.definition: an establishment where people gather to play billiards or pool, usually equipped with multiple tables and cues for players

examples.x: The poolroom offered snacks and drinks for players during matches.
They held a tournament at the neighborhood poolroom every month.
Friends gathered at the poolroom to unwind after work.
He hustled at the poolroom, winning several games in a row.
The poolroom was filled with the sounds of balls clacking and players chatting.

sense.definition: a facility where golfers practice their swings and shots, usually with multiple tees and targets for aiming practice

examples.x: He spent an hour at the driving range improving his swing technique.
The driving range has covered bays for practicing in any weather.
Beginners often start at the driving range to learn the basics of golf.
The golf coach gives lessons at the driving range to improve players' skills.
The driving range offers a variety of distances for different skill levels.

sense.definition: a designated area or facility where skateboarders and other wheeled sports enthusiasts can practice and perform tricks

examples.x: The city recently opened a new skate park in the downtown area.
Skateboarders of all skill levels gather at the skate park to practice their tricks.
The skate park features ramps, rails, and bowls for skaters to utilize.
Local authorities enforce safety rules at the skate park to prevent accidents.
He spends most afternoons at the skate park perfecting his ollies and kickflips.
